Title: Create a new drug order with all required attributes
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider with active visit for a patient; on the patient dashboard Orders tab
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Drug Order | 2. Search and select a drug from the formulary | 3. Enter dose and select dose unit | 4. Select route | 5. Select frequency | 6. Enter duration and duration unit | 7. Click Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Amoxicillin 500mg; Dose=500; Unit=mg; Route=Oral; Frequency=Twice daily; Duration=7; DurationUnit=Days
Expected_Result: Drug order is created with status Active, appears in the active orders list, and an order number is generated

Title: Reject drug order saved without a dose
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider with active visit; on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Select a drug from the formulary | 2. Leave the dose field empty | 3. Select route Oral | 4. Select frequency Once daily | 5. Click Save Order
Expected_Result: Order is not saved; a validation error indicates dose is required and the form remains open

Title: Reject drug order with missing route
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider with active visit; on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Select a drug | 2. Enter a valid dose | 3. Leave route unselected | 4. Select frequency | 5. Click Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Metformin 500mg; Dose=500; Unit=mg; Route=(empty); Frequency=Twice daily
Expected_Result: Order is not saved; a validation message indicates route is required

Title: Free-text (non-coded) drug order capture
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; formulary does not contain the requested drug
Test_Steps: 1. Click Add Drug Order | 2. Choose the free-text drug option | 3. Type the drug name as free text | 4. Enter dosing instructions | 5. Click Save Order
Test_Data: FreeTextDrug=Compounded magic mouthwash; Instructions=Swish and spit 10ml four times daily
Expected_Result: Order is saved as a non-coded drug order and is clearly flagged as free text in the orders list

Title: Dose at the minimum allowed boundary is accepted
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; drug with configured min dose of 1 mg
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Enter the minimum valid dose | 3. Select unit mg | 4. Complete route and frequency | 5. Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Warfarin; Dose=1; Unit=mg; Route=Oral; Frequency=Once daily
Expected_Result: Order is accepted at the lower boundary value with no dosing error

Title: Dose just below minimum is rejected or warned
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; drug with configured min therapeutic dose
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Enter a dose below the minimum threshold | 3. Complete route and frequency | 4. Attempt to Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Warfarin; Dose=0.05; Unit=mg; Route=Oral; Frequency=Once daily
Expected_Result: System warns the dose is below the safe range and requires acknowledgement or blocks the order

Title: Dose at the maximum allowed boundary is accepted
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; drug with configured max single dose
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Enter the maximum valid dose | 3. Complete route and frequency | 4. Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Paracetamol; Dose=1000; Unit=mg; Route=Oral; Frequency=Four times daily
Expected_Result: Order is accepted at the upper boundary with no error

Title: Dose above maximum triggers overdose warning
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; drug with configured max single dose and max daily dose
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Enter a dose exceeding the maximum single dose | 3. Complete route and frequency | 4. Attempt to Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Paracetamol; Dose=5000; Unit=mg; Route=Oral; Frequency=Four times daily
Expected_Result: System raises a maximum dose exceeded / overdose alert and requires explicit override with reason or blocks save

Title: Reject negative dose value
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Enter a negative dose | 3. Complete route and frequency | 4. Attempt to Save Order
Expected_Result: Order is rejected with a validation error that dose must be a positive number

Title: Reject non-numeric dose entry
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Type alphabetic characters into the dose field | 3. Attempt to Save Order
Expected_Result: Dose field rejects non-numeric input or validation prevents save

Title: Pediatric weight-based dose calculation
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; pediatric patient with recorded weight; weight-based dosing configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Drug Order for the pediatric patient | 2. Select a weight-based drug | 3. Enter dose per kg | 4. Confirm the system computes total dose from patient weight | 5. Save Order
Expected_Result: Total computed dose equals 500 mg and is displayed for confirmation before saving

Title: Block weight-based order when patient weight missing
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; pediatric patient with no recorded weight
Test_Steps: 1. Open Add Drug Order | 2. Select a weight-based drug requiring weight | 3. Enter dose per kg | 4. Attempt to Save Order
Expected_Result: System prompts that patient weight is required for this calculation and prevents saving until weight is recorded

Title: Major drug-drug interaction alert is raised
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient already has an active warfarin order; interaction checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Add a new drug order for an interacting drug | 2. Complete dose route frequency | 3. Click Save Order | 4. Observe interaction alert
Expected_Result: A drug-drug interaction alert of Major severity is displayed identifying both drugs and the clinical risk

Title: Provider override of interaction alert with reason is audited
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; interaction alert raised for the new order
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a major interaction alert | 2. Choose to override the alert | 3. Select or enter an override reason | 4. Confirm and Save Order | 5. Review the audit log
Test_Data: NewDrug=Aspirin; OverrideReason=Benefit outweighs risk, monitoring INR
Expected_Result: Order is saved, the override and reason are recorded with provider identity and timestamp in the audit trail

Title: No interaction alert for non-interacting drug pair
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has an active order for a drug with no known interaction with the new drug
Test_Steps: 1. Add a new drug order for a non-interacting drug | 2. Complete the order details | 3. Save Order
Expected_Result: Order saves without any drug-drug interaction alert

Title: Decision table: interaction severity to system action mapping
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; interaction engine configured with severity tiers
Test_Steps: 1. For each severity tier place an interacting order | 2. Observe required action: Contraindicated blocks, Major requires override+reason, Moderate requires acknowledgement, Minor informational only | 3. Verify save behavior matches tier
Expected_Result: Each severity tier produces the mapped system action exactly as defined in the decision table

Title: Contraindicated combination is hard-blocked
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient on an MAOI; contraindicated pairing configured
Test_Steps: 1. Add an order for a drug contraindicated with the active MAOI | 2. Complete details | 3. Attempt to Save Order
Expected_Result: System hard-blocks the order with no override path and explains the contraindication

Title: Allergy alert when ordering a drug the patient is allergic to
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has a documented penicillin allergy on the allergy list
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order for a penicillin-class drug | 2. Complete details | 3. Click Save Order | 4. Observe allergy alert
Expected_Result: A contraindication alert is shown citing the documented penicillin allergy and reaction severity before the order can proceed

Title: Cross-sensitivity allergy alert for related drug class
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient allergic to penicillin; cross-sensitivity rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order for a cephalosporin | 2. Complete details | 3. Save Order | 4. Observe cross-sensitivity warning
Expected_Result: A cross-sensitivity warning is displayed advising potential reaction risk

Title: Override allergy alert requires reason and is audited
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; allergy alert raised
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an allergy contraindication alert | 2. Select override | 3. Enter an override justification | 4. Save Order | 5. Inspect audit log
Test_Data: OrderedDrug=Amoxicillin; OverrideReason=Prior tolerance verified, mild rash only
Expected_Result: Order saves and the allergy override with reason, provider, and timestamp is persisted to the audit trail

Title: No allergy alert when patient has no documented allergies
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient allergy list explicitly marked No Known Allergies
Test_Steps: 1. Add any drug order | 2. Complete details | 3. Save Order
Expected_Result: Order saves without an allergy contraindication alert

Title: Select a valid administration frequency
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Open the frequency picker | 3. Select a standard frequency | 4. Complete remaining fields | 5. Save Order
Expected_Result: Selected frequency is persisted and shown in the order summary

Title: PRN (as-needed) order with indication
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Mark the order as PRN | 3. Enter the PRN indication | 4. Complete dose route frequency | 5. Save Order
Expected_Result: Order is saved as PRN with the indication captured and displayed

Title: Reject zero or negative duration
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Enter duration of zero | 3. Complete other fields | 4. Attempt to Save Order | 5. Repeat with a negative duration
Expected_Result: System rejects zero and negative durations with a validation error

Title: Scheduled future start date order
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Add a drug order | 2. Set a scheduled start date in the future | 3. Complete details | 4. Save Order
Expected_Result: Order is saved with status Scheduled and activates on the chosen start date

Title: Revise an active drug order creates a new revision
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has an active drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active order | 2. Choose Revise/Modify | 3. Change the dose | 4. Save the revision | 5. Review order history
Expected_Result: A new order revision is created linked to the previous order, the prior order is voided/superseded, and the active dose is now 500 mg

Title: Discontinue an active drug order with reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has an active drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active order | 2. Select Discontinue/Stop | 3. Enter a discontinue reason | 4. Confirm | 5. Verify order status
Expected_Result: Order transitions to Stopped/Discontinued with the reason and timestamp recorded; it no longer appears as active

Title: Discontinue requires a reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has an active drug order; reason mandatory
Test_Steps: 1. Open the active order | 2. Select Discontinue | 3. Leave the reason blank | 4. Attempt to confirm
Expected_Result: System prevents discontinuation and requires a reason to be entered

Title: Cannot discontinue an already stopped order
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has a previously stopped drug order
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order history | 2. Locate a stopped order | 3. Attempt to discontinue it again
Expected_Result: Discontinue action is unavailable or rejected for an already-stopped order

Title: Order state transitions: New to Active to Stopped
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; ability to place, activate, and stop orders
Test_Steps: 1. Create a new order and verify status New/Active | 2. Confirm it appears in active list | 3. Stop the order | 4. Verify it moves to Stopped | 5. Confirm history retains all states
Expected_Result: Order moves through each valid state, invalid transitions are not permitted, and full history is preserved

Title: Order with refills decrements remaining refills on each fill
Preconditions: Logged in as Pharmacist; an active order configured with refills
Test_Steps: 1. Open the order with refills remaining | 2. Process a dispense/fill | 3. Verify refills remaining decremented | 4. Repeat until exhausted
Expected_Result: Each fill decrements the remaining refill count; after the third the count reaches zero

Title: Block dispense when no refills remain
Preconditions: Logged in as Pharmacist; order with zero refills remaining
Test_Steps: 1. Open an order with zero refills remaining | 2. Attempt to process another fill
Expected_Result: Dispense is blocked and the pharmacist is informed that authorization or a new order is required

Title: Dispense medication against an active order
Preconditions: Logged in as Pharmacist; active drug order pending dispense; stock available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the pharmacy dispensing queue | 2. Select the pending order | 3. Confirm drug dose and quantity | 4. Record dispense quantity | 5. Confirm dispense
Expected_Result: Dispense is recorded, inventory decrements, and the order shows a dispense event with quantity and dispenser identity

Title: Block dispense quantity exceeding ordered amount
Preconditions: Logged in as Pharmacist; active order with a defined total quantity
Test_Steps: 1. Open a pending order | 2. Enter a dispense quantity greater than the ordered total | 3. Attempt to confirm
Expected_Result: Dispense is blocked or flagged because quantity exceeds the authorized amount

Title: Partial dispense leaves remaining quantity outstanding
Preconditions: Logged in as Pharmacist; active order; insufficient stock for full quantity
Test_Steps: 1. Open a pending order | 2. Record a partial dispense quantity | 3. Confirm | 4. Verify remaining quantity
Expected_Result: Partial dispense recorded and remaining quantity of 3 stays outstanding for future fill

Title: Schedule II controlled substance order requires extra authorization
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider with DEA/prescriber authority; controlled-substance workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Add an order for a Schedule II drug | 2. Complete dose route frequency | 3. Provide required prescriber credential / e-sign | 4. Save Order
Test_Data: Drug=Oxycodone; Schedule=CII; Credential=Provider DEA on file
Expected_Result: Order requires and validates prescriber authorization/e-signature before it can be saved

Title: Block controlled substance order from unauthorized prescriber
Preconditions: Logged in as a provider WITHOUT controlled-substance prescribing authority
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to add a Schedule II drug order | 2. Complete details | 3. Attempt to Save Order
Expected_Result: System blocks the order and records the unauthorized attempt; no order is created

Title: No refills permitted on Schedule II prescription
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; Schedule II order being created
Test_Steps: 1. Add a Schedule II drug order | 2. Attempt to set refills greater than zero | 3. Save Order
Expected_Result: System prevents authorizing refills on a Schedule II order per regulation

Title: Controlled substance dispense logged to audit trail
Preconditions: Logged in as Pharmacist; Schedule II order ready to dispense
Test_Steps: 1. Dispense the controlled substance | 2. Record quantity and witness if required | 3. Confirm | 4. Inspect the controlled-substance log
Expected_Result: Dispense is recorded in an immutable controlled-substance audit log with quantities, identities, and timestamps

Title: Create order surfaces as FHIR MedicationRequest
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; valid OAuth token; a drug order exists for the patient
Test_Steps: 1. Issue GET /MedicationRequest?patient={id} | 2. Inspect the returned resource | 3. Validate status, intent, medication, and dosageInstruction fields
Expected_Result: Response returns a valid R4 MedicationRequest with status=active, intent=order, medicationCodeableConcept, and dosageInstruction matching the order

Title: FHIR MedicationRequest dosage uses valid coded units
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; a drug order with structured dosing exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET the MedicationRequest | 2. Inspect dosageInstruction.doseAndRate | 3. Verify unit code system (UCUM) and value
Expected_Result: doseAndRate value is 500 with UCUM unit code mg from the correct code system URI

Title: Reject malformed FHIR MedicationRequest POST
Preconditions: FHIR API enabled; valid token
Test_Steps: 1. POST a MedicationRequest missing required medication element | 2. Inspect HTTP response and OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: API returns HTTP 400/422 with an OperationOutcome describing the missing required element

Title: Outbound RDE^O11 pharmacy order message generated
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured; pharmacy order placed
Test_Steps: 1. Place a new drug order | 2. Capture the outbound HL7 message at the interface | 3. Parse the message type and RXO/RXE segments
Expected_Result: A well-formed RDE^O11 message is emitted with correct RXO/RXE segments reflecting the order

Title: Inbound dispense status RDS^O13 updates order
Preconditions: HL7 interface configured; an order awaiting dispense status
Test_Steps: 1. Send an inbound RDS^O13 dispense message | 2. Verify the order dispense status updates | 3. Confirm RXD segment quantity is reflected
Expected_Result: Order reflects the dispense status and quantity from the inbound HL7 message

Title: Malformed HL7 order message is rejected and logged
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured with validation
Test_Steps: 1. Send an HL7 message with a corrupt MSH segment | 2. Observe interface handling | 3. Check the error/dead-letter log
Expected_Result: Message is rejected, not processed, and the error is logged for reprocessing without crashing the interface

Title: REST API create drug order returns created resource
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid session/token; active encounter exists
Test_Steps: 1. POST to the drug order endpoint with valid payload | 2. Inspect HTTP status and body | 3. GET the created order to confirm persistence
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with the created order including a uuid, and the order is retrievable

Title: API rejects drug order without active encounter
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid token; no active encounter for patient
Test_Steps: 1. POST a drug order payload referencing a patient with no active encounter | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: API returns a 4xx error indicating an encounter is required and no order is created

Title: Unauthenticated API request to orders is denied
Preconditions: REST/FHIR API reachable; no auth credentials supplied
Test_Steps: 1. Issue a GET to the orders endpoint with no auth header | 2. Inspect the response status
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized and discloses no patient or order data

Title: Nurse role cannot create a prescriber-only drug order
Preconditions: Logged in as a Nurse without prescribing privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to the patient orders | 2. Attempt to create a new drug order | 3. Observe available actions
Expected_Result: Create drug order action is unavailable or denied based on role privileges

Title: Order view access is logged for PHI audit
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; audit logging enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open a patient medication order | 2. View order details | 3. Inspect the access/audit log
Expected_Result: Access to the patient medication data is recorded with user, patient, action, and timestamp

Title: Drug order persisted with referential integrity
Preconditions: Backend DB access for verification; an order was just created via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Create a drug order in the UI | 2. Query the orders table by order uuid | 3. Verify foreign keys to patient, encounter, and drug concept resolve
Expected_Result: Order row exists with valid non-null foreign keys to patient, encounter, and drug, and matches UI values

Title: Voided order retains row with voided flag set
Preconditions: Backend DB access; an order was discontinued/voided via UI
Test_Steps: 1. Void/discontinue an order in the UI | 2. Query the order row | 3. Verify voided flag, void reason, voided_by, and date_voided
Expected_Result: Row is retained (soft delete) with voided=true, void reason, voided_by, and date_voided populated

Title: Pairwise coverage of route x frequency x formulation
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise-selected combination create an order | 2. Vary route, frequency, and formulation per the pairwise set | 3. Save and verify acceptance
Expected_Result: Each valid pairwise combination is accepted and persisted with the correct attributes

Title: Add Drug Order form is keyboard and screen-reader accessible
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; screen reader available; on the Add Drug Order form
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate the entire form using only the keyboard | 2. Verify focus order is logical | 3. Verify each field has an accessible label | 4. Trigger a validation error and confirm it is announced
Expected_Result: All fields are reachable and labeled, focus order is logical, and validation errors are announced to assistive technology

Title: Exploratory session on complex polypharmacy order entry
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient with 8+ active medications, multiple allergies and interactions
Test_Steps: 1. Add several interacting and allergy-triggering orders | 2. Mix PRN, scheduled, taper, and weight-based orders | 3. Probe alert stacking, override flows, and order list rendering | 4. Note any defects or confusing behavior
Expected_Result: No crashes, alerts are accurate and not suppressed incorrectly, overrides are audited, and any anomalies are documented

Title: Maximum cumulative daily dose across multiple orders
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has an existing acetaminophen-containing order
Test_Steps: 1. Note the existing acetaminophen daily total | 2. Add a second acetaminophen-containing product | 3. Confirm cumulative daily dose calculation | 4. Observe max-daily-dose alert
Expected_Result: System detects the cumulative daily dose exceeds the safe limit and raises a duplicate-therapy/max-daily-dose alert

Title: Duplicate therapy alert for same drug already active
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient already has an active order for the same drug
Test_Steps: 1. Add a new order for a drug the patient is already taking | 2. Complete details | 3. Save Order | 4. Observe duplicate therapy alert
Expected_Result: A duplicate therapy alert is shown identifying the existing active order

Title: Renal-adjusted dose alert for impaired renal function
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient with documented low eGFR; renal dosing rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Add a renally-cleared drug order at a standard dose | 2. Complete details | 3. Save Order | 4. Observe renal dosing guidance
Expected_Result: System raises a renal dosing alert recommending dose adjustment for the reduced eGFR
